input.initialPrompt: Analyze this project for incremental build issues. Identify any custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed.

data.reasoningText: Let me analyze the project files to identify incremental build issues.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

Both custom targets in `IncrementalBroken.csproj` will **re-run on every build** even when nothing has changed:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6)
- Writes `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` to a file on every build.
- Since the timestamp always changes, the file content always differs, which **invalidates downstream caching** and forces `CoreCompile` to re-run.
- **Fix:** Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes to the target, or remove it entirely. Embedding a timestamp in the build is fundamentally anti-incremental.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13)
- Has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild **always considers it out of date**.
- It unconditionally runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and rewrites `git-hash.g.cs` every time—even if the hash hasn't changed—which also invalidates `CoreCompile`.
- **Fix:** Add `Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects)"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"`, or compare before overwriting so the file timestamp only changes when the content actually differs.

### Summary

| Target | Root Cause |
|--------|-----------|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Output always changes (current time) |
| `EmbedGitHash` | No `Inputs`/`Outputs`; unconditional rewrite |

Both targets lack the MSBuild incremental-build contract (`Inputs`/`Outputs`), ensuring they execute every time and forcing a full recompile regardless of whether source files changed.
